pattern: Working inward from both ends and greedily walking a matching character into place with adjacent swaps -- falling back to nudging a lone unmatched character toward the center when no partner exists yet -- counts exactly the swaps a palindrome arrangement requires.
hints: Work with two pointers starting at the two ends of the string; whatever character currently sits at the left pointer, look for its matching partner walking in from the right pointer.
Once you find a matching character, bring it next to the right pointer using adjacent swaps (each swap counts as one move), then shrink the range and repeat.

If no partner is found before the search reaches the left pointer itself, that character is the eventual odd-length middle character -- swap it one position to the right (counting as one move) and retry the same left pointer.

edge_inputs.description: six-character case with an odd middle letter
single character is trivially already a palindrome
already a palindrome, zero moves needed
one letter needs to travel across the whole string
long-distance repeated pairs requiring several swaps to interleave
every character identical, already a palindrome

stress.description: randomly shuffled multiset of lowercase letters guaranteed convertible to a palindrome (at most one letter has an odd count)
stress.note: doubling every counted letter and adding at most one singleton guarantees the palindrome precondition
